code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test24-APR-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria) are abnormal and may reflect renal dysfunction and/or metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c and normal serum glucose do not support chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for acute or chronic kidney disease and possible metabolic causes.
